---
title: "EFFECT-3 · Phase 3 Efficacy and Safety Report"
subtitle: "A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Trial in Advanced Solid Tumors"
author: "EFFECT-3 Study Group"
date: "2026-05-08"
format:
  html: { toc: true, toc-depth: 2 }
  pdf: { documentclass: article, geometry: margin=1in }
---

## Abstract

**Background.** Standard-of-care therapy for stage IIIB/IV refractory solid tumors yields median overall survival (mOS) of 8.4 months. EFFECT, a small-molecule inhibitor, demonstrated mOS 12.6 mo in Phase 2.

**Methods.** Multicenter, double-blind, 1:1 randomization (active vs placebo), n=250, primary endpoint OS at 12 mo.

**Results.** 12-mo OS rate: 62% (active) vs 38% (placebo); HR 0.62 (95% CI 0.48–0.81), p<0.001. Grade ≥3 AE: 24% active vs 19% placebo.

**Conclusion.** EFFECT extends overall survival with manageable toxicity in this advanced-tumor population.

## CONSORT Flow Diagram

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
  A[Assessed for eligibility · n=412] --> B{Excluded · n=162}
  B --> C[Did not meet criteria · 98]
  B --> D[Declined · 41]
  B --> E[Other · 23]
  A --> F[Randomized · n=250]
  F --> G[Active arm · n=125]
  F --> H[Placebo · n=125]
  G --> I[Analyzed · n=123]
  H --> J[Analyzed · n=121]
```

## Methods

### 2.1 Study population
- Adults ≥18 with biopsy-confirmed stage IIIB/IV solid tumor refractory to first-line.
- ECOG 0-1, adequate organ function.

### 2.2 Endpoints
- **Primary**: 12-mo OS rate.
- **Secondary**: PFS, ORR (RECIST 1.1), QoL (EORTC QLQ-C30).
- **Safety**: CTCAE v5.0.

### 2.3 Statistical analysis plan
Stratified log-rank test, two-sided α=0.05, power 80% to detect HR 0.65. ITT analysis.

## Results

### 3.1 Baseline characteristics (Table 1)

| Characteristic | Active (n=125) | Placebo (n=125) |
|----------------|---------------|-----------------|
| Age, median (range) | 62 (38–78) | 64 (40–81) |
| Female, n (%) | 58 (46%) | 61 (49%) |
| ECOG 0, n (%) | 71 (57%) | 68 (54%) |
| Tumor types: NSCLC | 38% | 41% |
| ··  CRC | 24% | 22% |
| ··  Other | 38% | 37% |

### 3.2 Efficacy

**Primary endpoint**: 12-mo OS active 62% vs placebo 38%, HR **0.62** (95% CI 0.48–0.81), p<0.001 (stratified log-rank).

PFS: median 8.1 vs 4.2 mo, HR 0.51 (0.39–0.66), p<0.001.

ORR: 34% vs 12% (p<0.001 by chi-square).

### 3.3 Safety

| AE Grade ≥3 | Active | Placebo |
|-------------|--------|---------|
| Any | 30 (24%) | 24 (19%) |
| Neutropenia | 12 | 6 |
| Fatigue | 8 | 4 |
| Diarrhea | 5 | 3 |
| ALT elevation | 4 | 2 |
| Discontinuation due to AE | 9% | 5% |

No treatment-related deaths.

## Discussion

EFFECT meets the primary endpoint with clinically meaningful 24-percentage-point improvement in 12-mo OS. Toxicity profile manageable; neutropenia is dose-dependent and reversible.

Limitations: 12-mo follow-up; longer-term survival under analysis. Subgroup analyses (Section 4) suggest benefit consistent across tumor types except subtype X (HR 0.91, n=18, underpowered).

## Conclusions

EFFECT demonstrates statistically and clinically significant survival benefit. Submission to FDA planned Q4 2026.

## Appendix

Detailed Kaplan-Meier curves, AE listings, and statistical analysis plan provided in supplementary appendix.
